data.title: MANTRA’s 88% crash sparks over $71 million in liquidations: Coinglass
data.description:
The abrupt crash of MANTRA (OM) has triggered over $71.8 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, with the token plunging more than 88%.

Specifically, 10 positions each saw liquidations exceeding $1 million, according to Coinglass data. The liquidation data are based on publicly available data — which may understate the true extent of market liquidations.

The token of MANTRA, a Layer 1 blockchain focused on real-world assets, suddenly tanked about 10% within an hour on Sunday before plummeting from $5.21 to its current value of around $0.74 — down 88.2% in the past 24 hours — according to The Block's MANTRA price page.

John Patrick Mullin, the project's co-founder, said in an X post that OM's drastic price movements were triggered by "reckless forced closures initiated by centralized exchanges on OM account holders."

"The timing and depth of the crash suggest that a very sudden closure of account positions was initiated without sufficient warning or notice," Mullin added.

The Block has reached out to Mullin and MANTRA for further comment.

Last year, a Hong Kong court ordered six individuals associated with MANTRA DAO to disclose financial records related to the project following a lawsuit alleging they had misappropriated DAO assets. The defendants argued that the assets are owned by token holders.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2025 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: Broad gains lift the STOXX
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 gains 2%
  • Bank, tech, energy lead
  • Tariff exemptions lift mood
  • Nasdaq futures rise

BROAD GAINS LIFT THE STOXX

The tariff reprieve on smartphones and other electronics is lifting European shares at the open on Monday in a broad-based bounce that's pushing the STOXX 600 up by around 2%.

Tech (.SX8P) stocks are among the strongest gainers, in good company with banks (.SX7P), energy (.SXEP) and miners (.SXPP). Bond-proxy utilities (.SX6P) were relative laggards, up 0.6%.

Over 95% of the STOXX constituents are up and no subsector was trading in the red. Here is your opening snapshot:

(Danilo Masoni)

*****

EARLIER ON LIVE MARKETS:

BEFORE THE BELL: TECH CHARGES UP, LVMH KICKS OFF EARNINGS CLICK HERE

DOING THE TARIFF TWO-STEP CLICK HERE

data.title: Danone Target Raised to EUR73 From EUR66 by Citi
Danone Cut to Neutral From Buy by Citi
Greaves Cotton Appoints Group CEO
data.description:
Engineering company Greaves Cotton has appointed Parag Satpute as managing director and group chief executive officer of the company, according to a Monday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

He will be responsible for Greaves Engineering, Greaves Retail, Excel Controlinkage, and Greaves Technologies, effective from Monday.

data.title: Bourses' stocks are 'good places to hide', Exane says; cuts Euronext on valuation
data.description:
** Exane BNP Paribas says stock exchanges and information service companies are "good places to hide" in times of uncertainty, but downgrades Euronext after record gains

** "We see risks that trends are not sustainable, and that velocity of trading falls back," it says about Euronext

** The Paris-listed stock has gained more than 26% YTD and more than 73% since the beginning of 2024

** Exane's top choice is Deutsche Boerse , as the stock offers defensive characteristics and is also exposed to structural growth

** It says London Stock Exchange Group has less attractive momentum, trading in line with U.S. information services peers, but it might benefit from the rollout of some Microsoft initiatives

data.title: Oil Edges Higher As Traders Weigh Trump's Shifting Signals on TariffsMarket Talk
data.description: Oil prices tick higher as traders weigh the Trump administration's mixed signals on tariffs and China's commodity imports data. Brent crude and WTI are both up 0.3% to $64.95 and $61.66 a barrel, respectively. The U.S. exempted electronics like smartphones and computers from so-called reciprocal tariffs, but said these products might soon face separate levies as part of an investigation into semiconductors. Trump's tariffs pushed oil to a four-year low last week amid concerns over global economic growth and energy demand. Meanwhile, China's crude imports rebounded in March, with investors now waiting for the release of key economic indicators this week. "These data points will be key in assessing oil demand prospects as the tariff standoff with the U.S. continues," says Razan Hilal, market analyst at Forex.com. Traders also await OPEC's monthly oil-market report due later on Monday. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Latest on U.S. Politics
data.description:
Market Talks covering the impact of U.S. Politics and White House policies on companies and markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0345 ET - Oil prices tick higher as traders weigh the Trump administration's mixed signals on tariffs and China's commodity imports data. Brent crude and WTI are both up 0.3% to $64.95 and $61.66 a barrel, respectively. The U.S. exempted electronics like smartphones and computers from so-called reciprocal tariffs, but said these products might soon face separate levies as part of an investigation into semiconductors. Trump's tariffs pushed oil to a four-year low last week amid concerns over global economic growth and energy demand. Meanwhile, China's crude imports rebounded in March, with investors now waiting for the release of key economic indicators this week. "These data points will be key in assessing oil demand prospects as the tariff standoff with the U.S. continues," says Razan Hilal, market analyst at Forex.com. Traders also await OPEC's monthly oil-market report due later on Monday. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0343 ET - President Trump's decision to pause many planned import tariffs confirms the administration's willingness to come to a deal with Europe, France's finance chief says. "It confirms there is space for negotiation with the Trump administration," Eric Lombard tells newspaper Ouest-France in an interview. Trump last week said he would pause the bulk of his planned tariffs, which he calls reciprocal, on goods from many trading partners including the EU, though not China. "I was pleasantly surprised that Trump changed stance so quickly," Lombard says, noting that the immediate effect of tariffs would be to drive down growth in the U.S., and drive up inflation there. But it is too soon yet to declare victory, Lombard says, given that some tariffs remain in place. "For that reason, Europe must keep up the pressure," he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0333 ET - European tech stocks rise after the Trump administration exempted some tech products from the so-called reciprocal tariffs on Friday. Shares in Infineon Technologies, STMicroelectronics and Logitech jump 3.3%, 4% and 5.1%, respectively. Meanwhile, the Stoxx Europe 600 Technology Index opened 2.8% higher in early European trading. The exemptions for tech-related products will likely provide short-term relief for the sector, Jefferies analyst Charles Brennan says in a note. However, "this level of policy volatility isn't helpful for business confidence and ultimately to orderly decision making," Brennan adds. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0322 ET - The dollar's depreciation on President Trump's tariff polices could lead to lower inflation for U.S. trading partners, Commerzbank's Thu Lan Nguyen says in a note. "If this remains the case, most central banks will be spared a dilemma: weighing up economic and inflation risks," she says. That could allow for more interest-rate cuts that would reduce the appreciation pressure of non U.S. currencies against the dollar. For the time being, it seems highly unlikely that the effect of the tariffs will be reversed, she says. The DXY dollar index falls 0.9% to 99.2280 after hitting a three-year low of 99.014 Friday. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0233 ET - The dollar stays lower after hitting a three-year low Friday on building concerns over the U.S. economic fallout of President Trump's tariff plans. Trump on Sunday said there would be no exemptions in tariffs after confusion over levies on electronics. The incoherence in U.S. economic policy coupled with reduced credibility caused by Trump's constant U-turns is "clearly creating more than a few jitters," Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. This has eroded a huge amount of trust in the U.S., he says. "De-dollarization is now a real, and frankly scary, prospect." The DXY dollar index falls 0.7% to 99.4490 after hitting a low of 99.3510 Friday. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0206 ET - LBBW is comfortable with its forecasts for the 10-year German Bund yields, says senior fixed income analyst Elmar Voelker. The German bank has left its forecast unchanged at 2.80% at end-2025 and at 3.00% at mid-2026, he says in a note. German Bunds outperformed U.S. Treasurys last week as investors sought European alternatives to weakening U.S. assets in the tariff turmoil. Last week, the 10-year Bund yield moved between a high of 2.720% and a low of 2.432%, having finished the week little changed at 2.534%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0155 ET - A pause in cuts is no longer an option for the European Central Bank's rate setters, ING's Carsten Brzeski says. U.S. tariffs on European goods, and Washington's more generally protectionist policy under President Trump, have revived growth fears for a eurozone that appeared to have a rosier horizon in the shape of planned European investment in defense and infrastructure, Brzeski tells investors in a note. "The strengthening of the euro as well as the drop in energy prices, has added to the disinflationary forces the current trade tensions will have for the eurozone," he says. The ECB, which looked to be mulling a pause in its recent series of cuts to interest rates, will have no choice but to continue to ease rates when its governing council meets Thursday in Frankfurt, he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0130 ET - Miners' quarterly operational results, due over the next few weeks, "have lost salience in the face of global risk-off and recessionary fears," Citi analysts say in a note. A roughly 30% selloff in stocks from year-to-date highs suggests that mining stocks are beginning to price in a tariff-induced shock to global growth, the analysts say. They don't expect upcoming quarterly results to give investors much reason for cheer. "We see the operational trends for Q1 25 to be soft, with copper and iron ore production likely declining sequentially but staying ahead of the last-year levels," they say. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0129 ET - The Trump administration's latest tariff exemptions on electronic devices is a sign of "major de-escalation" of Trump's tariff program, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. Given this walkback, Jefferies expects China not to implement the rule change that would impose 125% tariff on chips manufactured in the U.S. but packaged elsewhere. Trump will likely use ADR delisting and U.S. investment ban policies as leverage to negotiate with China, they say. "But if the U.S. and China start to negotiate, given the walkback on tech tariffs, the chance these risks would materialize would be much lower," they say. A negotiation could bring the theme of Chinese AI back into investors' focus, they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0116 ET - Investors should hold off buying 'risk-exposed' commodities such as oil and base metals, according to Citi analysts. Investors should wait "until the 'Fed put' or a material 'Trump put' kicks in," they say. "Or until copper reaches $7,500/ton or we see a major U.S. shale or OPEC+ response, whichever is first." The analysts reckon it is entirely possible that a Fed put, such as rate cuts or quantitative easing, happens before a significant 'Trump put.' The latter could include the removal of all reciprocal tariffs until tax cuts are enacted and there is time for dealmaking with other countries, they say.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0050 ET - Shares of North American base metals producers are likely to remain volatile until there is a trade deal between the U.S. and China, Jefferies analyst Christopher LaFemina says in a note. Stocks in those companies rose 13% on average last Wednesday, tumbled Thursday, and then rallied again Friday, he says. "Even when this gets resolved, the over-reliance of mining on China justifies low valuations," says LaFemina, adding that higher commodity prices and earnings would be essential for a strong recovery. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0038 ET - China's retaliatory tariffs on the U.S. could benefit domestic chip manufacturers making mature nodes, Citi analysts say in a research note. China's Semiconductor Industry Association last week has made suggestions on how to define the origin of semiconductor imports. According to its suggestion, integrated circuits made in the U.S. and packaged in China could still be subject to Beijing's 125% reciprocal tariff, the analysts say. Therefore, Beijing's tariffs favor domestic suppliers at the expense of American integrated device manufacturers such as Texas Instruments. U.S companies could shift to manufacturer chips in China or elsewhere to avoid the import tariff, they say. Citi expects chip production to become increasingly localized due to rising geopolitical tensions.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Philippines less vulnerable to global trade shocks, says economic minister
data.description:
The Philippines’ relatively low exposure to trade compared to its Asian neighbours offers a buffer against global economic shocks, Economic Planning Secretary Arsenio Balisacan said on Monday.

"The economy is not as vulnerable to shocks in the global marketplace as our neighbours... because the Philippine economy's exposure to trade is fairly small," Balisacan told a press conference.

However, he cautioned against complacency, stressing the importance of strengthening export performance by diversifying markets and addressing investment constraints so the country could take advantage of trade diversion opportunities resulting from the sweeping U.S. tariffs.

"We need to double, even triple, our efforts to improve the investment environment so investors see the Philippines as a viable destination," Balisacan said.

The Philippines has not been spared from the global trade wars triggered by U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs, with Washington threatening levies on Filipino exports.

The Philippines, which already has free-trade agreements with countries such as South Korea and Japan, is actively pursuing a similar pact with the United States to safeguard and expand market access.

Balisacan said it was premature to revise the country’s economic targets despite the heightened uncertainty. He noted that resilient domestic consumption, which makes up around three-quarters of GDP, should continue to support growth.

He believes hitting the lower end of this year's 6.0% to 8.0% growth target remains realistic.

data.title: France's Vallourec rises on Serimax divestment
data.description:
** Shares in Vallourec rise 4.8% after the metallurgical group said it has entered exclusive talks with investment company Aldebaran to sell Serimax, its unit focused on welding solutions, for 79 million euros ($90 million)

** "We think it's an opportunistic divestment and it was not expected by the market as management previously stated that Dusseldorf land sale was the last 'big divestment' expected form the company," says Midcap Partners analyst Julien Thomas

** 7 million euros of the total divestment value will be paid via earn-out

** "The price tag is elevated and given the net cash situation of the company, one should expect that the proceed will be distributed to shareholder," Thomas adds

($1 = 0.8775 euros)

data.title: Pakistan Remittances Hit Record High
data.description:
Pakistan’s remittances hit a record $4.1 billion in March 2025, a 37% yearly increase, signaling economic recovery helped by IMF support.

Key sources were Saudi Arabia ($987 million), UAE ($842 million), UK ($684 million), and the US ($420 million).

Strong remittance growth reflects improved confidence as Pakistan stabilizes after avoiding default in 2022 with IMF loans.

The central bank raised its remittance forecast for FY25 to $38 billion (from $36 billion) and expects forex reserves to reach $14 billion by June.

Reduced dollar demand and a crackdown on informal currency trading have helped steady the rupee, down just 0.85% over the past year.

The country also reported a current account surplus so far this fiscal year.

Total remittances for July–March FY25 are up 33% to $28 billion from $21 billion in the same period last year.

data.title: BioStock: Green light to continue Elicera's CARMA-trial in B-cell lymphoma
data.description:
Elicera Therapeutics

Elicera Therapeutics has received the green light from the Data Safety and Monitoring Board (DSMB) to continue its phase I/IIa clinical trial, CARMA, evaluating the CAR T-cell therapy candidate ELC-301 for B-cell lymphoma. Armed with immune-activating properties via the company’s iTANK platform, the therapy is a key component of Elicera’s broader immunotherapy strategy. The approval comes as the company prepares to present preliminary efficacy data from the first patient cohort in May.

Read the article at biostock.se:

https://www.biostock.se/en/2025/04/green-light-to-continue-eliceras-carma-trial-in-b-cell-lymphoma/

This is a press release from BioStock - Connecting Innovation & Capital. https://www.biostock.se/

https://news.cision.com/elicera-therapeutics/r/biostock-green-light-to-continue-elicera-s-carma-trial-in-b-cell-lymphoma%2Cc4135490

data.title: YCIC Eco-Technology's Consortium Chosen as First Selected Bidder for 21 Billion Yuan Highway Reconstruction
data.description:
A consortium with YCIC Eco-Technology was selected as the first preferred bidder for a 21.1 billion yuan highway reconstruction, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Saturday.

The reconstruction and expansion will cover the Shilin to Kunming Section of the G78 Shankun Expressway / G80 Guangkang Expressway in China.

The construction period will run for four years, the landscaping services provider said.

The company's shares closed over 3% higher.

data.title: REG - Watches of Switzlnd. - Update to £25m share buyback programme
data.description:
RNS Number : 8769E Watches of Switzerland Group PLC 14 April 2025  

Watches of Switzerland Group PLC

Update to £25m share buyback programme

Watches of Switzerland Group PLC (the "Company") today announces that further to the £25m share buyback programme originally announced on 10 March 2025, the Company has entered into an updated agreement with Barclays Bank PLC, acting through its investment bank ("Barclays"), in relation to the purchases of ordinary shares of £0.0125 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") which now includes buybacks during closed periods of the Company (in addition to open periods) (the "Share Buyback Programme"). The maximum aggregate consideration for the Share Buyback Programme remains £25,000,000, from 10 March 2025.

The agreement allows Barclays, acting as riskless principal, to purchase, during open and closed periods of the Company, up to 23,957,029 Ordinary Shares. These share purchases will be made on the Company's behalf, in accordance with the agreement and, in the case of any purchases made during closed periods, shall be made independently of and uninfluenced by the Company.

Since the commencement of the Share Buyback Programme, to 11 April 2025 (inclusive), 2,249,429 shares have been repurchased for an aggregate consideration of £9,291,038. The Share Buyback Programme will continue under the Group's clear and disciplined approach to capital allocation, and end no later than the earlier of (i) the date of the Company's next annual general meeting; or (ii) the close of business on 3 December 2025.

Any share purchases effected pursuant to the agreement will be subject to its terms and, in any case, will be effected in a manner consistent with the general authority vested in the Company to repurchase shares at the Company's annual general meeting on 3 September 2024, the Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014 and the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 (both as incorporated into UK domestic law by the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018) and the Financial Conduct Authority's Listing Rules. 

The Company will announce any market repurchase of ordinary shares no later than 7.30 a.m. on the business day following the calendar day on which the repurchase occurred. Following the purchase of Ordinary Shares, they will be cancelled.  The sole purpose of these share purchases is to reduce the Company's share capital.

This announcement does not constitute, or form part of, an offer or any solicitation of an offer for securities in any jurisdiction. 

Contacts

The Watches of Switzerland Group

Anders Romberg, CFO                                                                          +44 (0) 207 317 4600

Caroline Browne, Group Finance and Investor Relations Director        +44 (0) 116 281 7420

investor.relations@thewosgroup.com

Headland

Lucy Legh / Rob Walker / Scarlett Hateley                                            +44 (0) 203 805 4822

wos@headlandconsultancy.com

About the Watches of Switzerland Group

The Watches of Switzerland Group is the UK's largest luxury watch retailer, operating in the UK, US and Europe comprising seven prestigious brands; Watches of Switzerland (UK and US), Mappin & Webb (UK), Goldsmiths (UK), Mayors (US), Betteridge (US), Analog:Shift (US) and Hodinkee (US), with a complementary jewellery offering. From 8 May 2024, the Group also owns the exclusive distribution rights for Roberto Coin in the USA, Canada, Central America and the Caribbean.

As at 26 January 2025, the Watches of Switzerland Group had 217 showrooms across the UK, US and Europe including 95 dedicated mono-brand boutiques in partnership with Rolex, OMEGA, TAG Heuer, Breitling, TUDOR, Audemars Piguet, Longines, Grand Seiko, Roberto Coin, BVLGARI and FOPE and has a leading presence in Heathrow Airport with representation in Terminals 2, 3, 4 and 5 as well as seven retail websites.

The Watches of Switzerland Group is proud to be the UK's largest retailer for Rolex, OMEGA, Cartier, TAG Heuer and Breitling watches.

www.thewosgroupplc.com

data.title: Chinese Shares Ended Higher, Led by Bank StocksMarket Talk
data.description: Chinese shares ended higher, with the Shanghai Composite Index rising 0.8% to 3262.81. The Shenzhen Composite Index was 1.15% higher and the ChiNext Price Index gained 0.3%. Chinese President Xi Jinping begins his three-nation trip to Southeast Asia amid intensifying trade tensions over the past few days. Investors are closely watching for further developments in U.S.-China trade relations. Among major stocks, banks led the gains, with Bank of Communications rising 2.6% and Bank of China adding 1.1%. Decliners included SMIC, which was 1.0% lower, and Midea, which dropped 0.8%.(tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Southeast Cement Unit Re-elects Chairperson
data.description:
Southeast Cement's major unit, Southeast Topgood re-elected Chen Min Duan as chairperson.

Chen also chairs Southeast Cement, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: North Macedonia seeks bids for Ohrid wastewater treatment plant
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), April 14 (SeeNews) - North Macedonia's transport ministry said it has launched a tender for the construction of a main collector and wastewater treatment plant in the village of Trpejca in the municipality of Ohrid.

The planned contract for the works will have a duration of two years, the transport ministry said in a notice published on the country's public procurement portal on Friday.

Interested parties can apply until May 12. The value of the procurement has not been disclosed.

data.title: Baota Industry Scraps Share Issuance Plan to Acquire Ningxia Electric Power, Resolves to Pay Acquisition Via Cash
data.description:
Baota Industry terminated plans to issue shares to acquire Ningxia Electric Power Investment New Energy from Ningxia Electric Power Investment Group, according to Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Saturday.

The Chinese roller bearings manufacturer will continue to acquire Ningxia Electric Power but will pay through cash instead of shares.

The company will also exclude the Sun Mountain Wind Power Phase III and IV projects, which are owned by Ningxia Electric Power, from its targeted assets to prevent uncertainty in subsidy recovery.

Shares of the company closed 6% higher.

data.title: Japan Posts Steepest Annual Decline in Japanese Nationals
data.description:
Japan's population of Japanese nationals fell by a record 898,000 to 120.3 million as of Oct. 1, 2024, government data showed.

The drop marks the steepest annual decline on record and extends a 13-year trend of accelerating contraction.

The overall population stood at 123.8 million, down 550,000, with foreign residents increasing by 342,000. The number of Japanese residents fell by 2,000-the first decline in two years.

A natural decrease of 890,000 marked the 18th consecutive year of more deaths than births. The share of people aged 65 and over rose to 29.3%, while children under 15 made up a record-low 11.2%.

Only Tokyo and Saitama saw population growth. Eighteen prefectures, including Akita and Aomori, posted declines of over 1%.

All prefectures recorded natural declines. Net migration was positive in 24, led by Tokyo, Osaka, and Chiba.
